records.ety: [OE. stubbe, AS. stub, styb; akin to D. stobbe, LG. stubbe, Dan. stub, Sw. stubbe, Icel. stubbr, stubbi; cf. Gr. <?/.]

defs: The stump of a tree; that part of a tree or plant which remains fixed in the earth when the stem is cut down; -- applied especially to the stump of a small tree, or shrub.
quotes: Stubs sharp and hideous to behold.
And prickly stubs instead of trees are found.

defs: A log; a block; a blockhead.
The short blunt part of anything after larger part has been broken off or used up; hence, anything short and thick; as, the stub of a pencil, candle, or cigar.
A part of a leaf in a check book, after a check is torn out, on which the number, amount, and destination of the check are usually recorded.
A pen with a short, blunt nib.
A stub nail; an old horseshoe nail; also, stub iron.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stubbed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Stubbing.]

defs: To grub up by the roots; to extirpate; as, to stub up edible roots.
quotes: What stubbing, plowing, digging, and harrowing is to a piece of land.

defs: To remove stubs from; as, to stub land.
To strike as the toes, against a stub, stone, or other fixed object.
